Coordinate descent for weighted Gaussian regression with sequential strong rule

cd_wgaussian_fit_ssr.Rd

Coordinate-descent algorithm for penalised weighted Gaussian regression with sequential strong rule (SSR) screening over a sequence of penalty values.

Usage

cd_wgaussian_fit_ssr(
  X,
  y,
  wt,
  multiplier,
  penalty,
  lam,
  alpha,
  gamma,
  lam_max,
  max_iter,
  eps,
  dfmax
)

Arguments

X

A numeric matrix of predictors (n x p).

y

A numeric response vector of length n.

wt

A numeric vector of observation weights (length n).

multiplier

A numeric vector of per-variable penalty multipliers (length p). Variables with multiplier = 0 are treated as unpenalised.

penalty

A string specifying the penalty: "LASSO", "MCP", or "SCAD".

lam

A numeric vector of penalty values, sorted in decreasing order.

alpha

A numeric scalar for the elastic net mixing parameter.

gamma

A numeric scalar for MCP and SCAD penalties.

lam_max

A numeric scalar for the maximum lambda value (used in SSR cutoff).

max_iter

An integer specifying the maximum total iterations allowed.

eps

A numeric scalar for the convergence tolerance.

dfmax

An integer specifying the maximum number of selected variables.

Value

A list with the following elements:

beta0

A numeric vector of intercepts for each lambda.

beta

A matrix of coefficients (p x length(lam)).

dev

A numeric vector of weighted residual sums of squares.

eeta

A matrix of fitted linear predictors (n x length(lam)).

df

A numeric vector of degrees of freedom for each lambda.

iter

An integer vector of iteration counts for each lambda.

On this page
